issue.body:
## Context

`pd-book-tools v0.14.0` shipped `py.typed`; downstream consumers no longer get `reportMissingTypeStubs` on `pd_book_tools` imports. However, during the 2026-05-23 pd-ocr-labeler-spa basedpyright cleanup (commit [c708eb4](https://github.com/ConcaveTrillion/pd-ocr-labeler-spa/commit/c708eb4)), the implementing agent added `# pyright: ignore[reportAny]` to roughly six call sites that access `Page.lines`, `Page.words`, and `Page.ground_truth_text` via `getattr()`. The agent's diagnosis: *"pd_book_tools lacks attribute-level type stubs for these fields; structural duck-typing is intentional."*

## Contradicting evidence

Running `uv run basedpyright pd_book_tools` from inside this repo reports `0 errors, 0 warnings` (modulo a separate cupy ignore-unnecessary-comment artifact tracked elsewhere). If attribute typing were genuinely missing, internal basedpyright should also flag it. So one of the following must be true:

1. The attribute types are properly annotated and propagate to consumers — the labeler-spa fixes are **over-suppression**, and the underlying smell is `getattr()` used where direct attribute access would type-check cleanly. (`getattr()` always returns `Any` regardless of upstream typing.)
2. The attribute types exist in source but don't survive the wheel build / `py.typed` flow correctly — the wheel ships the marker but loses annotations along the way.
3. The labeler-spa source variable upstream of the `getattr()` is annotated `Any` (e.g. via a deserialization or `dict` path) and the `getattr()` is just inheriting that `Any`-ness, not creating it.

## Reproduction

Write a small consumer file that installs the published `pd-book-tools` wheel (not the editable in-tree source) and does:

```python
from pd_book_tools.ocr.page import Page

def use(p: Page) -> int:
    return len(p.lines)        # case A: direct attribute access
    # return len(getattr(p, "lines"))  # case B: getattr access
```

Run `uv run basedpyright` against the consumer and compare:

- If case A reports `reportAny`, the wheel is shipping incomplete annotations → fix in pd-book-tools.
- If case A is clean and case B reports `reportAny`, `getattr()` is the smell → document workspace guidance and refactor labeler-spa.
- If both are clean from a wheel install but the labeler-spa repro shows the warning, an upstream variable in labeler-spa is poisoning the type chain → fix in labeler-spa.

## Affected attributes per the labeler-spa agent report

- `Page.lines`
- `Page.words`
- `Page.ground_truth_text`
- (possibly more — agent did not enumerate exhaustively; check the six `# pyright: ignore[reportAny]` sites added by commit [c708eb4](https://github.com/ConcaveTrillion/pd-ocr-labeler-spa/commit/c708eb4))

## Acceptance criteria

- A short reproducer that demonstrates which of the three hypotheses applies.
- A decision recorded in this issue:
  - Add attribute-level type fixes in `pd_book_tools` (and confirm they ship in the wheel), **or**
  - Document `direct attribute access > getattr()` as a workspace convention and reopen the labeler-spa fix to drop the unnecessary ignores.
- Cross-check pd-ocr-cli and pd-prep-for-pgdp for the same `getattr()` pattern. If absent, this is a labeler-spa code-smell concentration; if present, it reinforces option 1 or 2 above.

## Related

- pd-ocr-labeler-spa commit `c708eb4` — added the six ignores under investigation.
- pd-book-tools v0.14.0 release notes — `py.typed` shipped here.
- pd-book-tools v0.14.1 release notes — typing/override compat.

nodes.body:
Investigation done — **hypothesis 3 confirmed**. Not a pd-book-tools issue.

Research doc: [docs/research/2026-05-23-issue-206-attribute-typing-investigation.md](https://github.com/ConcaveTrillion/pd-book-tools/blob/main/docs/research/2026-05-23-issue-206-attribute-typing-investigation.md) (commit 401863f).

Findings:

1. `pd_book_tools` attributes (`Page.lines`, `Page.words`, `Word.ground_truth_text`) are fully typed with return annotations; direct access on a `Page`-typed variable produces zero basedpyright warnings.
2. The `reportAny` warnings in pd-ocr-labeler-spa came from `getattr()` itself — `getattr()` always returns `Any` regardless of how well the receiver is typed. This is intrinsic to Python's type system, not a pd-book-tools deficiency.
3. The wheel `pd_book_tools-0.14.1` ships `py.typed` and full annotated source — hypothesis 2 (annotations lost in wheel build) is ruled out.
4. Root cause: `PageLoadOutcome.payload: Any` in labeler-spa's `core/page_state.py` (an intentional placeholder pending M3 `PageRecord`) flows through `_resolve_page_object` / `_resolve_page_object_for_pages` resolvers typed as `object | None` rather than `Page | None`. The `getattr()` was a workaround for that gap, not for missing upstream typing.
5. Neither pd-ocr-cli nor pd-prep-for-pgdp use `getattr()` for these attributes — the pattern is a labeler-spa concentration.

Closing as "not planned" — labeler-spa follow-up to narrow the resolvers will be filed separately.

nodes.description: Design issue with a spec doc
nodes.name: kind:spec

issue.title: Investigate downstream reportAny on pd_book_tools attribute access from py.typed-aware consumers

timeline.body:
Investigation done — **hypothesis 3 confirmed**. Not a pd-book-tools issue.

Research doc: [docs/research/2026-05-23-issue-206-attribute-typing-investigation.md](https://github.com/ConcaveTrillion/pd-book-tools/blob/main/docs/research/2026-05-23-issue-206-attribute-typing-investigation.md) (commit 401863f).

Findings:

1. `pd_book_tools` attributes (`Page.lines`, `Page.words`, `Word.ground_truth_text`) are fully typed with return annotations; direct access on a `Page`-typed variable produces zero basedpyright warnings.
2. The `reportAny` warnings in pd-ocr-labeler-spa came from `getattr()` itself — `getattr()` always returns `Any` regardless of how well the receiver is typed. This is intrinsic to Python's type system, not a pd-book-tools deficiency.
3. The wheel `pd_book_tools-0.14.1` ships `py.typed` and full annotated source — hypothesis 2 (annotations lost in wheel build) is ruled out.
4. Root cause: `PageLoadOutcome.payload: Any` in labeler-spa's `core/page_state.py` (an intentional placeholder pending M3 `PageRecord`) flows through `_resolve_page_object` / `_resolve_page_object_for_pages` resolvers typed as `object | None` rather than `Page | None`. The `getattr()` was a workaround for that gap, not for missing upstream typing.
5. Neither pd-ocr-cli nor pd-prep-for-pgdp use `getattr()` for these attributes — the pattern is a labeler-spa concentration.

Closing as "not planned" — labeler-spa follow-up to narrow the resolvers will be filed separately.

issue.body:
## Context

Six `# pyright: ignore[reportAny]` comments were added in commit [c708eb4](https://github.com/ConcaveTrillion/pd-ocr-labeler-spa/commit/c708eb4) on call sites that do `getattr(page, "lines"|"words"|"ground_truth_text")` over values resolved through `_resolve_page_object` / `_resolve_page_object_for_pages`.

The pd-book-tools investigation ([pd-book-tools#206](https://github.com/ConcaveTrillion/pd-book-tools/issues/206), closed) confirmed these warnings are **not** an upstream typing gap — `pd_book_tools.ocr.page.Page` is fully annotated. The `reportAny` is coming from labeler-spa's own type plumbing:

- `core/page_state.py` — `PageLoadOutcome.payload: Any` (intentional placeholder pending M3 `PageRecord`)
- The `_resolve_page_object*` resolvers return `object | None` / `Any | None` instead of `Page | None`
- Downstream callers fall back to `getattr()` because the static type isn't `Page`

## Proposed fix

Add an `isinstance(lift_result, Page)` guard at the lift boundary inside the resolver functions and narrow their return signature to `Page | None`. Then the six call sites become direct typed attribute access (`page.lines`, `page.words`, `page.ground_truth_text`), and the six `# pyright: ignore[reportAny]` comments can be removed.

## Acceptance criteria

- `_resolve_page_object` / `_resolve_page_object_for_pages` return `Page | None`.
- The 6 `# pyright: ignore[reportAny]` comments from commit c708eb4 are deleted.
- `make ci` green (0 errors, 0 warnings).
- PR description notes whether M3 `PageRecord` work subsumes this or whether the narrowing should land standalone first.

## Related

- pd-book-tools#206 — investigation that ruled out upstream cause (closed not-planned).
- Workspace CONVENTIONS rule "basedpyright — fix the warning, don't suppress it" (added 2026-05-23) — flags `getattr()` on typed objects as a high-confidence violation.

repository.description: FastAPI + React/Vite/TS replacement for the NiceGUI pd-ocr-labeler

issue.title: Narrow lift resolvers to Page | None to drop reportAny ignores from c708eb4
